must_fix: Blocking: packages/db/src/telegram-egress.ts lists stale status='sending' rows as ready for retry and markSending reclaims stale sending rows, while apps/core/src/runtime/runtime-lifecycle.ts replays those rows through telegram.sendMessage. A process crash or stall after the Bot API request reaches Telegram but before markSent persists leaves the row in sending; after the stale window the retry worker can issue a second Bot API send for the same action_id. This still violates AC-F0029-13 and the duplicate-prevention NFR. Fix by making ambiguous in-flight sending rows non-resendable until reconciled/manual recovery, or by splitting the claim/transport protocol so only rows proven to have never reached Bot API are automatically retried.
evidence: packages/db/src/telegram-egress.ts:299 markSending permits stale status='sending' rows; packages/db/src/telegram-egress.ts:387 listReadyToRetry selects stale status='sending' rows

apps/core/src/runtime/runtime-lifecycle.ts:280 replayReadyTelegramEgress routes ready rows back through the bounded gateway; apps/core/src/actions/tool-gateway.ts:1350 performs Bot API send after the send claim
